title: Where to See the Berlin Wall - Quick Summary
items: **Most of the 155 km Wall is gone** — torn down by Berliners after 1989 and systematically demolished by 1992. Less than 3 km of original concrete survives in the city today.
**East Side Gallery** is the longest remaining stretch: 1.3 km of original Wall covered in 100+ murals along the Spree River, near Warschauer Str. Completely free.
**Berlin Wall Memorial on Bernauer Straße** is the most authentic site — both walls and the death strip are preserved. Free documentation center with a viewing tower.
**Checkpoint Charlie** is the most famous crossing point — but the guardhouse is a replica. The free outdoor exhibition boards tell the story clearly; skip the paid museum.
**Topography of Terror** has a 200 m original section and one of Berlin's best free museums — built on the former Gestapo and SS headquarters site.
**Look down as you walk through Berlin** — a double row of cobblestones embedded in the pavement marks the Wall's former course across the entire city.
